name: Military History
description: Battles, wars, and the generals who fought them — from Thermopylae to D-Day.

questions.q: In what year was the Spanish Armada defeated by the English?

source.notes: Off the coast of England under Queen Elizabeth I.

questions.q: Who defeated Napoleon at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815?
At which Greek mountain pass did 300 Spartans famously hold off the Persians in 480 BCE?

source.notes: King Leonidas led the Greek force; the Persians ultimately took the pass.

questions.q: Which 1066 battle established William the Conqueror's Norman rule over England?
Which 1485 battle ended the Wars of the Roses and brought the Tudors to power?

source.notes: Henry Tudor (Henry VII) defeated Richard III. 'A horse, a horse, my kingdom for a horse!'

questions.q: Which 1944 amphibious assault was the largest seaborne invasion in history?
Which 1815 Battle of New Orleans was fought after the War of 1812 had officially ended?

source.notes: Treaty of Ghent (Dec 1814) hadn't reached the U.S. yet. Andrew Jackson became a hero.

questions.q: In which 1876 battle did General Custer's 7th Cavalry meet defeat against the Sioux and Cheyenne?

source.notes: Sitting Bull and Crazy Horse led the victorious tribes.

questions.q: In what U.S. state is the site of the Battle of Little Bighorn?
Which Pacific battle, June 4-7, 1942, is considered the turning point of WWII in the Pacific?
Which 1942 Egyptian battle did the Allies (under Montgomery) decisively win?

source.notes: Second Battle of El Alamein (Oct-Nov 1942) drove Rommel out of Egypt.

questions.q: What was the name of Germany's strategy of fast, combined-arms warfare in WWII?

source.notes: 'Lightning war' — used in invasions of Poland, France, USSR.

questions.q: Who was the Roman general assassinated on the Ides of March in 44 BCE?
Which UK air battle of 1940 saw Churchill say 'Never was so much owed by so many to so few'?

source.notes: RAF defeated the Luftwaffe in summer/autumn 1940.

questions.q: Which 1982 war pitted the UK against Argentina over disputed South Atlantic islands?

source.notes: Lasted 74 days; British victory under Margaret Thatcher.

questions.q: Which Vietnam War battle's surprise coordinated assault during a holiday is widely seen as the war's turning point?

source.notes: January 30, 1968 - North Vietnamese surprise attack during the Tet (lunar new year) ceasefire.

questions.q: Which 1854 cavalry charge during the Crimean War became famous through Tennyson's poem?

source.notes: Battle of Balaclava, October 25, 1854. 'Theirs not to reason why...'

questions.q: What was the codename for the Allied D-Day invasion of Normandy?

source.notes: Operation Husky was the invasion of Sicily.

questions.q: Which 1781 American Revolution battle ended in British surrender to Washington?

source.notes: Cornwallis surrendered to Washington and Rochambeau on October 19, 1781.

questions.q: Which 1916 Western Front battle was one of the deadliest in human history, with ~1 million casualties?

source.notes: July-November 1916. ~57,000 British casualties on day one alone.
